title: US Considers FINRA-Like Watchdog for Top AI Models
summary: The Trump administration is considering creating an independent watchdog agency modeled after FINRA to review the safety of top AI models, led by Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and currently under review by Chief of Staff Susie Wiles. This move aims to address Wall Street&\#x27;s cybersecurity concerns and Silicon Valley&\#x27;s dissatisfaction with temporary government controls, giving both industries more say in safety standards. If implemented, it could establish a new paradigm for AI regulation in the US. The plan aligns with a suggestion from Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis for an industry-funded independent regulator. Anthropic and OpenAI have previously objected to government demands to modify or delay model releases. The framework is still under discussion and has not been reviewed by President Trump.
context: FINRA \(Financial Industry Regulatory Authority\) is a self-regulatory organization that oversees brokerage firms and enforces rules to protect investors, operating under the SEC&\#x27;s supervision. It was formed in 2007 from the merger of NASD and NYSE regulatory units. The proposed AI watchdog would similarly be an independent, industry-funded body that sets and enforces safety standards, reporting to a government agency.
